description: The European Union passed a landmark directive in 2019 to ban several single-use plastic products, such as straws and cutlery, by 2021. The proposal saw high public support and faced minimal corporate organized opposition due to the clear availability of alternatives.

key_events.description: Media campaigns highlight the 'Great Pacific Garbage Patch' and plastic-choked oceans.
European Commission formalizes the directive, garnering widespread backing from all member states.
Large retailers preemptively switch to sustainable packaging, normalizing the change.

calibration_notes: An example of a 'low friction' environmental policy with broad consensus across stakeholder groups.
